source.definition: The first month of the year in the Gregorian calendar.

explanation.sourceText: The English word "January" comes from the Latin 'Ianuarius', named after Janus, the Roman god of doors and beginnings.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „January” provine din latinul 'Ianuarius', numit după zeul roman Janus, zeul ușilor și începuturilor.

target.definition: Prima lună a anului în calendarul gregorian.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word "ianuarie" derives directly from the Latin 'Ianuarius', named after the Roman god Janus.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „ianuarie” provine direct din latinul 'Ianuarius', numit după zeul roman Janus.

examples.sourceText: The new year begins in January.
January is the first month of the year.
examples.targetText: Ianuarie este prima lună a anului.

learningTips.text: Remember that "January" starts with a 'Jan' sound, just like the Romanian "ianuarie" starts with 'ia', helping you link the two words.
Think of Janus, the Roman god of doors and beginnings, to recall that January is the start of the year.
The English word "January" and Romanian "ianuarie" both come from Latin, so their similarity can help you remember the Romanian month name.
Connect "ianuarie" with the Romanian word "an" (year) to reinforce the idea that January is the first month of the year.
